title: Polymath flows-lite workflow
description: Workflow YAML schema for polymath-flows v0.1. Serial step replacement / insertion only. Unsupported keys must fail closed.

then.description: Partial workflow: composes a parent at build time via `polymath-flow flatten`. Must contribute at least one composition section; name/version/steps are optional (inherited from the parent when absent).

description.description: One-sentence summary of what the workflow does.

whenToUse.description: One-line, model-facing routing hint: when this workflow is the right multi-step arc to propose. Mirrors a skill description. Surfaced verbatim in the SessionStart workflow-index, so keep it tight. Schema-optional (so external/partial workflows still validate), but REQUIRED for in-repo workflows by the WORKFLOW-2 conformance gate (build-workflow-index.py --strict).

triggers.description: 3-5 naive user phrasings (no workflow name) that should surface this workflow. Consumed by tools/build-workflow-index.py and used as the falsification anchor for workflow-triggering tests. Not injected into context. Trigger phrases must be unique across ALL workflows (enforced by the WORKFLOW-2 gate). Schema-optional but required for in-repo workflows.

detectionSignals.description: Deterministic, machine-checkable hints that raise this workflow's prior during detection. Not injected into context; consumed by the detect step to disambiguate proposals between colliding workflows.

paths.description: File globs / artifact paths whose presence in the request or repo hints this workflow.

intents.description: Short intent labels, input-presence markers, or keywords that hint this workflow.

chainsTo.description: Workflow names to propose as the natural next arc when this workflow completes (Phase 4 arc chaining), e.g. respondToIncident -> incidentRetroToActions. The executor surfaces these in the completion output; the agent PROPOSES them and never auto-runs. Targets must be real workflows (enforced by build-workflow-index.py --strict).

extends.description: Parent workflow reference in the form '<plugin>:<workflow>@<major.minor>'. BUILD-TIME ONLY: a partial declaring extends is composed into a standalone workflow by `polymath-flow flatten`; the runner hard-errors on any workflow that still carries extends/override/insertAfter at validate/start/next/assert time. The @major.minor pin must prefix-match the parent's semver. v0.1 supports serial step replacement and named-anchor insertion only.

provenance.description: Stamped by `polymath-flow flatten` on flattened output: the extends ref the file was built from, the parent's full version at flatten time, and a content hash over the canonical parent+partial sources. `polymath-flow flatten --check` recomputes the flatten and fails on drift. Hand-authored workflows must not declare this key.

requires.description: Hard dependencies. Workflow validation fails if any required plugin is not installed, or if a required capability has no provider configured in `.polymath/capabilities.yaml`. Either or both of `plugins` and `capabilities` may be declared; capabilities are the recommended shape for cross-provider work (issue tracker, observability, pager, etc.) and capability resolution adds the resolved adapter plugins to the effective plugin set transparently.

capabilities.description: Map of capability name (from registry/schemas/capabilities.schema.json) to either `true` (any provider OK) or a constraint object pinning a specific provider. The capability vocabulary is closed; declaring an unknown capability fails validation. Resolution looks up the configured provider in the project's `.polymath/capabilities.yaml`.

inputs.description: User-supplied inputs that the workflow expects at start time.

guards.description: Preconditions run at `start`, before any run state is created. A failing blocking guard refuses the start (status guard-failed, exit 2, no run directory); advisory failures and not-applicable outcomes are reported on the start payload and the run proceeds. stepSummaryMatches is not legal here — no step summaries exist before any step has run.

steps.description: Ordered serial steps. v0.1: each step runs after the prior step completes successfully.

invoke.description: Routing label of the form '<plugin>:<capability>', or 'agent:<plugin>:<name>' for a forked-context agent (plugins/<plugin>/agents/<name>.md; resolved by tools/check-workflow-invokes.py). v0.1 is a label, not a programmatic slash-command call. May contain `${capabilities.<cap>.plugin}` or `${capabilities.<cap>.provider}` placeholders so workflows can be provider-agnostic; placeholders are resolved at run time from the project's `.polymath/capabilities.yaml`.

prompt.description: Instruction the executor presents when reaching this step. May reference ${inputs.*} and ${workflow.*} placeholders.

needs.description: Step IDs that must be complete before this step. v0.1 expects a single predecessor (implicit serial order).

artifacts.description: Files this step is expected to produce or update. CHECKED at `complete` time: a step may only complete when every declared path exists, unless artifactsAdvisory is true.

artifactsAdvisory.description: When true, missing declared artifacts WARN at `complete` instead of refusing it — for genuinely conditional artifacts. Declared and auditable, never ambient.

topology.description: Composition pattern for this step (§ 4.6). 'series' executes after the previous step (default if absent). 'fanout' is accepted by validation but executor still runs serially in v0.1.5 — actual parallel execution lands when the runtime supports it. Other patterns reserved.

override.description: When 'extends' is set: replace parent steps with the same id (steps), and/or drop parent steps (removeSteps — applied last, guarded by the strong-gate-survival invariant; see docs/WORKFLOW-SCHEMA.md § 3).

insertAfter.description: When 'extends' is set, insert additional steps after a named anchor step id in the parent.

mustPass.description: Deterministic checks the executor runs after the last step. Failure pauses the workflow with a violation summary.

severity.description: advisory checks run and report but cannot fail the workflow on their own. blocking checks halt the workflow on failure. If omitted, defaults are: stepSummaryMatches→advisory; everything else→blocking. Independent of severity, appStarts may resolve 'not-applicable' — a non-blocking OUTCOME (not a severity) reported in the result's not_applicable array when the project declares no matching smoke recipe.

artifact.description: Artifact name (matches registry/schemas/artifacts/<name>.schema.json) for artifactValid / artifactSchemaStrict checks.

minBodyChars.description: For artifactSchemaStrict: minimum non-whitespace, non-heading characters in the body (after frontmatter). Catches hollow stub artifacts (e.g. '# PRD\n').

rejectAdditionalProperties.description: For artifactSchemaStrict: if true, reject any frontmatter field not declared in the artifact schema, regardless of the schema's own additionalProperties setting. Default true.

filesChanged.description: For diffConstraint: bound on number of files changed in the working tree (or since the given ref).

linesChanged.description: For diffConstraint: bound on total added+removed lines in the working tree (or since the given ref).

pathAllowlist.description: For diffConstraint: glob patterns; any changed path that does not match at least one allowlist entry fails the check.

pathBlocklist.description: For diffConstraint: glob patterns; any changed path that matches a blocklist entry fails the check.

since.description: For diffConstraint: git ref to diff against. Defaults to the working tree vs HEAD.

lang.description: For appStarts: which `smoke.<lang>` recipe from the frozen project-context snapshot to run (key matches `stack.languages[].lang`). Optional when the snapshot declares exactly one recipe. appStarts resolves 'not-applicable' (non-blocking, reported but never pausing) when no snapshot, no smoke block, or no recipe for this lang exists — a repo without boot-verification config is not penalised.

capability.description: For connectorAvailable: capability that must have a provider configured in `.polymath/capabilities.yaml` (and a resolvable adapter plugin). Pass/fail only — an unconfigured connector fails the check; use it in `guards` to stop a connector-dependent workflow before any work.

provider.description: For connectorAvailable: additionally require the configured provider to be exactly this one.
